vendor: The Edge (by Achieve IT Solutions / Aquifer; marketed as "The Edge for Jewelers" at theedgeforjewelers.com)
category: Jewelry-specific retail POS / store management (on-premise, Windows desktop; SQL backend)
region: USA / North America (dominant US independent-jeweler POS)

live_monitoring: Dashboards module: tile-based configurable graphical dashboards with 4 tile types — Numeric (single KPI like sales, repair pick-ups), Gauge (goal vs actual, e.g. care plans sold), Chart (pie/bar/doughnut: items sold by category or associate), List View (actionable task lists). Tiles can be scoped per-associate or store-wide; multiple named dashboards can be created.

Daily Activity Report: end-of-day reconciliation detailing each transaction by sale #, customer, goods $, services $, other $, subtotal, tax, total, tender, on-account $, and future (pending) value; groups by drag-and-prioritized fields.
Daily Activity Report surfaces Avg Sale per group for at-a-glance live performance.
Automated Notifications module: automatic text/email to customers when repairs/appraisals/special orders are ready, reducing staff burden (also feeds the live repair feed).

Tracking Service Jobs / Job Workload Management: live status of repairs & custom jobs flowing through the store (intake → in-work → pickup); associates can advise customers of exact job status in 'a couple of mouse clicks'.
Multi-store synchronization: robust transfer system maintains sync across 2+ stores/locations (Locations Feature) — the basis for a multi-store wall.
TraxSales integration: AI-on-chip people counting with demographic detection (age/gender) feeds foot-traffic into the system; pitched as 'manage your unsold customers' (traffic vs conversion).
sales_monitoring: Sales Comparison Report: period-over-period sales comparison.
Time & Day Report: retail sales broken down by day-of-week AND time-of-day — the core hourly/trend analytics view, pairs with TraxSales traffic for conversion analysis.
Salesperson Performance Report (see Workforce) doubles as channel/associate sales-performance analytics.
Frequently Sold Items report + Top 'N' Customer Report: best-seller and top-customer analytics.
Vendor Style Analysis Report & 20/80 vendor insight: official claim that stores discover ~20% of customers = 80% of sales, and sales concentrate in a handful of vendors; reports quantify this.
Automated Reports Feature: scheduled/emailed reports (memorized report definitions).
Tender Detail Report & Custom Tender Detail Report: payment-method mix analytics (cash/card/financing/store-account).
Sales Tax Report, Statements and Aging, Account Adjustment Report: fiscal sales monitoring.
Advertising Reports + Occasions Report (birthdays/anniversaries): marketing-effectiveness vs sales attribution.
Drive Retail & SnapRetail partner integrations: digital/print marketing that drives store traffic, attributable back into sales reports.
profit_intelligence: Per-item cost & sale price WITH margins visible in inventory; shows 'the lowest price you can accept' (floor-price / negotiation floor) when a customer haggles.

Gross Profit Performance in Salesperson report: GP $, GP% (profit as % of total sale), and % Markup (markup multiplier on RETAIL price, e.g. cost $48 → sell $100 = 52% GP = 109% markup). This is true jewelry COGS→margin intelligence.

Open to Buy (OTB) wizard: generates an entire purchasing schedule for an upcoming sales period from past data, sales estimates, current on-hand inventory, and on-order inventory; forecast weighted by the previous 3 years of buying periods; separates Total COGS, Special Orders COGS, Repeat Sales COGS, Net COGS, and (COGS) Sales estimate. This is forward profit/capital-allocation intelligence.

Aged Inventory Management System (AIMS): monthly run that applies tiered spiffs (bonus commissions) and markdowns at specific age thresholds to liquidate old stock; produces preview of Old vs New values, generates a Bonus Poster (staff-facing spiff signage) and in-case signage (customer-facing clearance). Direct margin-recovery tool.
Weekly Buying Plan: pairs with AIMS to enforce 'just in time' smaller/smarter reorders — profitability via inventory turn optimization.

Spot Metal Pricing: live spot pricing for gold/silver/platinum/palladium (Kitco-linked); at POS optionally prompts for current metal price (configurable timeout); each store metal mapped to a Metal Type + purity value (e.g. 10k = 41% gold); updating metal price reprices all relevant items instantly. Critical for true gold/metal COGS and dynamic pricing.
Inventory Buying Tools + Pricing Report + Item Pricing Wizard + Reprice Items: systematic margin & repricing toolset.
Vendor analysis reports: vendor-level profitability and sell-through to cut unprofitable lines.
Pre-Appraisals & Appraisals modules: valuation/insurance records tied to cost basis.
Trade In / Buying module with actionable reports for gold/estate buying profitability.
Returns reporting with $ GP lost to returns (profit leak visibility).

kpis: Gross Sales ($ and % of store total) by associate
Sales Quantity (# and % of store total) by associate
Average Retail Sale (ARS) per associate WITH +/- delta vs store average (the canonical Edge coaching KPI: e.g. ARS $1,278 vs store avg $555 = +$723)
Gross Profit $, Gross Profit % (GP as % of sale), and % Markup (markup multiplier on RETAIL price) — per associate and per item
Returns: $ Sales, # Sales, and $ GP lost to returns per associate
Selling Skills: total discount $, # discounted sales, avg $ discount per discounted sale, avg % discount per discounted sale, and discount Ratio (e.g. '1 in 5' sales discounted)
Daily: goods $, services $, other $, subtotal, tax, total, tender $, on-account $, future/pending value
Inventory age buckets + spiff/markdown actions applied (AIMS)
Open-to-Buy spend recommendation vs prior-3-year weighted COGS (Total/Special-Order/Repeat/Net)
Foot traffic (TraxSales) vs sales → conversion rate, by time-of-day and day-of-week
20/80 customer and vendor concentration (sales attribution)
Repair/custom-job throughput: intake → in-work → pickup, with ETA adherence and notification status
Care-plan attach count (Gauge tile goal vs actual)
Metal spot price delta (Kitco) feeding live item repricing
differentiators: Jewelry-deep data model from day one (2004): serialized fine-item pricing tracked purchase→sale; Geller SKUs for repair pricing; Stones Wizard; GIA report integration; consignment & memo items; joined vs assembly items — purpose-built, not generic-retail retrofitted.

AIMS (Aged Inventory Management System) is a category-unique workflow: automated tiered spiff+markdown repricing with staff-facing Bonus Poster and customer-facing case signage — turns dead stock liquidation into a gamified, margin-recovering monthly ritual.
Open to Buy wizard weighted on 3 prior years of COGS, splitting special-order vs repeat vs net COGS — sophisticated forward capital-allocation rare in SMB POS.
Spot Metal Pricing wired into POS with per-metal purity mapping and optional at-purchase prompt — native gold/custom-chain economics.
Salesperson Performance report's precise KPI definitions (ARS +/-, GP%, %Markup-on-retail, discount Ratio) are an industry-standard coaching template widely cited by Edge Retail Academy.

Closed-loop hardware + partner ecosystem: RFID, barcode, tag printers, Cardknox/MSG/Heritage payment processing, QuickBooks, Shopify/Econ, GemFind/JewelCloud, Clientbook, Podium, TraxSales people-counting, Drive/SnapRetail marketing, Luxsurance/Jewelers Mutual warranties, Montage care plans.
Money-back-first-year guarantee + 24h free webinar training + Connecticut HQ face-to-face + in-store training.
Owned consulting arm (Edge Retail Academy / ERA, co-founded David Brown) layered on the data — 'total solution' not just software.
worth_copying: Salesperson Performance Report KPI set verbatim (per-associate ARS +/- vs store avg, GP $/GP%/%Markup-on-retail, Returns $ GP lost, Selling-Skills discount $/count/avg-%/Ratio) — this is the gold-standard associate-coaching scoreboard; replicate it as Zevar's Workforce Intelligence live scoreboard.

AIMS-style automated aged-inventory workflow: monthly batch that previews Old-vs-New price, applies tiered spiffs (bonus commission to move old stock) AND markdowns, then auto-generates (a) staff-facing spiff/bonus posters and (b) customer-facing in-case clearance signage. This is a standout, copyable margin-recovery engine that no generic POS has.

Open-to-Buy wizard: forward purchasing schedule from prior-3-year weighted COGS, splitting Total/Special-Order/Repeat/Net COGS, factoring on-hand + on-order — build this as Profit Intelligence 'dynamic what-if / capital allocation' module.

Spot Metal Pricing model: per-metal purity mapping (10k=41%, etc.) + live Kitco-equivalent spot feed + optional POS prompt-with-timeout + instant repricing of all affected items. Zevar already has gold_purchase; wire live spot + purity-based dynamic pricing into both sales and gold-buy valuation.
Floor-price / 'lowest acceptable price' shown alongside margin in inventory and at POS — simple but powerful negotiation guardrail for jewelry haggling.
Configurable tile dashboards (Numeric / Gauge / Chart / List) scoped per-associate OR store-wide, with named multi-dashboards — adopt this exact taxonomy for Live Monitor + Workforce scoreboard.

TraxSales-style people-counting → traffic-vs-sales conversion, sliced by Time & Day Report (day-of-week × time-of-day heatmap) — core Sales Monitor conversion analytics; integrate a people-counter partner or camera-based counting.
Daily Activity Report's column set (goods/services/other/subtotal/tax/total/tender/on-acct/future) with drag-to-prioritize grouping + Avg-Sale-per-group — ideal end-of-day live reconciliation tile.
20/80 attribution surfacing: auto-detect and headline the ~20% of customers = 80% of sales and the handful of vendors driving most revenue — a cheap, high-impact Profit/Sales insight.
Automated customer notifications (SMS/email on repair/custom/appraisal ready) tightly coupled to job status — reduces staff calls and feeds the Live Monitor repair feed.

Repair intake model: barcoded envelope + Tasks/Parts/History tabs + per-task assignee + declared value + express/estimate-only flags + deposit-due-now + photo capture at intake — copy this field set into Zevar's repair doctype.
Repair SKU library + Geller's-Blue-Book-style standardized repair pricing — enables consistent, profitable repair quoting.
Memorized/Automated Reports (saved report definitions, scheduled email) — essential for owner daily/weekly cadence.
weaknesses: On-premise / locally-installed (NOT cloud): no web or mobile access outside the workstation; multi-store requires server-client sync — a major limitation vs cloud-native competitors and a clear opening for Zevar.
Steep learning curve + long/labor-intensive setup; reviewers warn the breadth of features requires extensive training and slow staff rollout.
High up-front pricing ($4,600 single seat / $5,700 three seats / ~$12,450 two-location six-seat) plus 15–20% annual support — capital-heavy vs SaaS subscription models.
Aging desktop UX: reports module only recently moved 32-bit→64-bit; UI described as traditional/predefined workflows; competitors (Jewel360, Lightspeed, Pavilion) frame Edge as rigid/legacy.
Repair tracking has been described (by competitor Jewel360) as partly paper-based / envelope-reliant rather than fully digital.
Credit-card-processing transparency is minimal on the vendor site (integrated but opaque terms).
Reports are heavy/static (desktop reporting framework) rather than real-time interactive dashboards — the Dashboards module is configurable but not a true streaming live wall.
Single-vertical focus only on independent owner-operated jewelers (1–5 stores, ≤~20 users); not built for chains, multi-region, or e-commerce-first operators — limits scalability story.
pricing_model: Perpetual on-premise license, tiered by workstation/location (single seat ~$4,600; 3-workstation ~$5,700; 2-location/6-workstation ~$12,450; additional store ~$6,750) PLUS 15–20% annual support/upgrades; hardware, training, and some integrations billed separately. Some directories cite a ~$3,500 starting one-time cost. NOT SaaS.
ux_patterns: Desktop-installed Windows app (not browser/cloud); mouse+keyboard or touch-screen; server-client for multi-register

Drag-and-drop prioritization of data fields in report grouping
Per-associate dashboards and 'Last Associate'/'Reassign Associate' features tie activity to people

live_monitoring: POS Summary report: sales, transactions, tenders, and POS-balance info by STATION for a selected period (a per-register till view) — closest thing to live register activity, but it is a run-on-demand report, not a real-time wall
Sales Journal report: daily sales-transaction detail with per-batch page breaks, class/discount/coupon/return/payout/tax summaries — used to 'verify bank deposits' for a period (reconciliation view)
Store Analysis report: totals by tender and transaction type for a period
Real-time inventory tracking marketed as identifying best-sellers/low performers 'right away rather than at end of season' (vendor claim; not a live event feed)
RICS>Checkout mobile POS app for peak-traffic / outdoor sales (queue-busting)
Offline-first POS: keeps transacting when network drops, auto-syncs on reconnect
NO true real-time command-center / live-alert / anomaly-detection / multi-store activity wall found — RICS is fundamentally batch/scheduled-report oriented, not a streaming live monitor
sales_monitoring: Sales Analysis report — rank by Quantity Sold, Net Sales, Profit, ROI, Turns, Inventory Value; Analyze By Supplier/Class/Custom Entry/Color; Report Type SKU Detail / Class Summary / Supplier Summary / Price Point Summary

Best/Worst Sellers report — Best Of Store/Supplier/Class/SKU; rank by Sell-Thru %, ROI, Turns, Profit, Net Sales, Qty; cutoff logic ('Worst Below a Cutoff Point', 'Best Above a Cutoff Point', 'Top N items'); minimum-quantity-sold floor
Sales Analysis Inventory Summary — beginning vs ending on-hand, plus sales/returns/transfers/adjustments/receipts in the period (period-over-period inventory bridge)
Multi-period comparison: Report Column 1 + Report Column 2 to compare two sales periods side-by-side; up to 4 sales periods selectable on Stock Status
Show Percentages By: 'Percent of Store' vs 'Percent of Report' (normalization choice for share-of totals)
Grid Analysis — best/worst selling CELL within a size/attribute grid (sell-thru and on-hand % of total per grid cell) — directly transferable to jewelry attribute matrices
Price Point Summary — sales distribution by price band (useful for basket/value analytics)
Store Options across reports: Separate Stores / Combine Stores / Compare Stores / Store Summary (built-in multi-store comparison mode)
Date/aging conditions: Date First Received, Date Last Received, Age By — to age inventory and find slow movers
profit_intelligence: Profit $ is an optional, toggleable column on Salesperson Analysis and Sales Analysis (opt-in, not first-class)
ROI metric = gross-profit dollars earned per dollar invested in inventory, based on present sales rate and average inventory for the period (defined in docs) — RICS exposes the formula
Turns = annual ratio of how many times inventory is sold and replaced (defined in docs)
Value Inventory At options: Active Selling Price / Average (weighted) Cost / Current Cost — lets the user recompute inventory value and margin under different cost bases
Inventory Summary Options: Quantity / At Cost / At Retail — margin/valuation lens
Pricing filters: 'Include only SKUs at Retail / at Markdown / with Perks (as of today)' — separates full-price vs promo vs perk sales for margin analysis
ROI & Turns and Costs are 'Also Print' add-ons on Stock Status and Best Sellers (i.e., profitability is a bolt-on, not the core lens)

NO: payment-processing-cost absorption, overhead absorption, gold/gemstone/labor COGS decomposition, dynamic what-if pricing, AI margin recommendations — RICS has none of these (it is a simple cost-vs-price Profit $ model)
workforce_intelligence: Salesperson Summary report — performance by Supplier or Class, period; Group By Supplier/Class; Class Depth Limit; Include SKU Subtotals; Include Cashier Summary; Separate/Combine Stores

Salesperson Analysis report — compares salespeople head-to-head for 'sales rewards and training-program adjustments'; supports Simple Ticket Count vs Calculated Ticket Count (qty>0=1, qty<0=-1, qty=0=0) to control how transactions are credited
Salesperson Add-On Performance report — measures a salesperson against a BASE class plus up to 3 ADD-ON classes (attachment-selling / UPT coaching tool, e.g. base=shoe, add-ons=socks/insoles/laces)
Accessories section: % of a salesperson's sales in a chosen class vs their total (category-mix coaching)

Multiple Sales Exclude: exclude a class (e.g. low-ticket add-ons) from the % of multi-item sales metric, so multi-sale/UPT rates aren't inflated by cheap items — a clever, transferable UPT-purity control
Sale-type filtering by salesperson: All Sales vs Special Orders vs Layaways separately
Time Clock report — clock-in/out records by salesperson (entry date/time) for hours compliance; Print Salesperson Detail per entry
User-status handling across all people reports: Include Unapproved / Disabled / Deleted Users (so offboarded staff still get credited/blamed in historical analysis)
Store Options Separate/Combine across all salesperson reports for cross-store associate comparison
NO native commission-calc engine, no quotas/targets-vs-actuals, no gamification, no scheduling/clock-in RULES enforcement, no coaching workflows — just the reports to feed those processes manually

Quantity Sold (units)
Profit $ (opt-in gross profit per item/SKU/period)
ROI (gross-profit $ per $ invested in inventory, at current sales rate & avg inventory)

On-Order At Once / Future / Total, In-Transit, Available (On-Hand + In-Transit)
Model vs On-Hand gap: Short, Critical (short AND not on order), Reorder, Max
Age (days between valuation date and first/last received date)
Sales History by Month (per item)
Ticket count: Simple vs Calculated (signed-quantity logic)
Salesperson sales by Supplier / by Class (with class-depth control)
GP% (shown in the per-item Info Report alongside monthly qty/$, ROI, Turns)
Tender mix and transaction-type mix (Store Analysis)
Per-station POS balance / till reconciliation (POS Summary)
ux_patterns: Parametric Report Creator: a single search-driven catalog (Reporting :: Report Creator) where every report shares the same File Options -> Report Options -> Report Criteria -> Printing Options -> Aging Options flow. Predictable, composable, trainable.

Persistent criteria vocabulary across all reports (By Store / State / Supplier / Class / Color / SKU / Col / Row / PO# / Coupon / Custom Entry) with comma-separated multi-select + per-field Exclude checkbox — users learn the filter grammar once.
Sort/Order selector ('In Order Of') baked into every ranking report: by Qty Sold / Net Sales / Profit / ROI / Turns / Inventory Value / Sell-Thru %.
'Analyze By' (dimension) x 'Report Type' (granularity: SKU Detail / Class Summary / Supplier Summary / Price Point Summary) x 'Group By' — three orthogonal axes for slicing the same dataset.
Store-Options toggle (Separate / Combine / Compare / Store Summary) appears on nearly every report — multi-store comparison is a first-class, ubiquitous control, not a separate screen.
Period-over-period via Report Column 1 / Report Column 2 (side-by-side sales-period comparison) and up to 4 sales periods on Stock Status.
Output-first: every report lets you pick File Type (incl. CSV w/ custom delimiter), name it, and Email-A-Copy-To at generation time — built for sharing/export, not just on-screen viewing.
Scheduled email distribution: assign any report to any person on any cadence ('as often or as little as you'd like') — ops-leadership digest pattern.
Opt-in richness via 'Also Print' checkboxes (Costs, ROI & Turns, Age, Sales History by Month, Location, Perks, Column Totals) — keeps the default report clean, power users layer on.
Grid (matrix) analytics: column/row model with % of total and sell-thru per cell — strong pattern for any attribute-matrix retail (transfers to jewelry karat/size/gem grids).
data_model_hints: SKU is the atomic item, extended by a GRID: column (Col) x row (Row) dimensions (built for apparel size matrices — Zevar would repurpose Col/Row for karat x size, or gem-type x setting).
Class hierarchy is multi-level with a 'Class Depth Limit' parameter (report at top class or drill to lowest leaf) — implies a tree structure on categories.
Supplier and Color are first-class item attributes; 'Product Custom Entries' are user-defined extra attributes (a generic extension point Zevar could use for metal/gem/cert).
Per-SKU pricing model carries: Retail Price, Markdown Price, and 'Perk' (a third pricing/promo layer) — three parallel price fields with 'as of today' report filters.
Per-SKU stock model carries: On-Hand, Model (target), Max, Reorder, plus derived Short (On-Hand - Model) and Critical (Short AND not on order) — a min/max/reorder-point replenishment model.
On-Order is split temporally: At Once (<=30 days) vs Future (>30 days) vs Total — the 30-day threshold is a store setting.

Inventory ledger uses an enumerated Inventory Change Type (Transfer Out/In, In-Transit, PO Receipt, Physical Count, Receipts w/out PO, Return to Supplier, Store Sales/COGS, Adjustments, Into/From Non-Sellable) — each transaction is typed; clean audit trail and a strong template for Zevar's stock ledger doctype.
Non-Sellable is a separate inventory state items move Into/From — useful pattern for damaged/dirty gold or pulled-for-repair stock.
Multi-store org has a Store Owner level that unlocks cross-store Report Criteria (implies role-tied data scoping); In-Transit (transferred-not-received) is its own quantity bucket.
User entity has statuses: Unapproved / Active / Disabled / Deleted, all reportable — lifecycle states on the salesperson/staff record.
Salesperson == Cashier (User Code) with optional separate crediting; tickets carry a Sale Type (Store / Special Order / Layaway / Employee / Return) at the Ticket Header.
Two ticket-counting modes (Simple count vs signed-quantity Calculated) implies line items carry a signed quantity and a transaction-type code on each ticket.

Frequent Buyer plan is a config entity: Calculation Type, Break Point (Qty or Amt), Accumulating Classes, min-price & max-markdown guards, Points Factor, Graduation Plan, Expiry + Grace, Redemption-on-current-vs-next — a flexible rules engine for loyalty, stored per-plan and referenced from the customer record.
Frequent Buyer accumulation/reward state lives on the customer record (deleting a plan nulls the customer's plan and wipes their accumulation/rewards).
differentiators: Deep, parametric specialty-retail reporting vocabulary (185+ reports) with a single consistent filter grammar shared across every report — unusually uniform and trainable for multi-store operators.

Best-in-class salesperson add-on/attachment analytics: explicit base-class + up-to-3-add-on-class report, plus a class-exclusion control to keep multi-sale/UPT rates honest — a genuinely smart UPT/attachment coaching tool.
ROI and Turns computed and exposed per SKU/salesperson with documented formulas, plus tri-mode inventory valuation (Active Price / Average Cost / Current Cost) on the same report.
Grid (matrix) analytics with per-cell sell-thru and % of total — purpose-built for attribute-matrix retail (sizes now; directly portable to jewelry karat/gem/size grids).

cross_cutting: Tiered analytics monetization: Basic Reporting (all plans) vs Advanced Reporting (Pro/Advanced/Enterprise, Core/Plus) vs Insights add-on (Plus, or Standard/Lean/Advanced/Enterprise with add-on). Lightspeed GATES its best analytics behind higher tiers — a packaging lesson (and a competitive opening for Zevar to ship deep analytics by default).
Saved + Scheduled reports: customize any sales/inventory/payments/tax report, save it, and schedule it (daily/weekly/monthly) to auto-email stakeholders. Strong 'report-as-a-deliverable' pattern.

Create Purchase Orders directly from inventory reports (one supplier at a time) — closes the loop from insight to action without leaving the reporting screen. Apply this 'insight -> action' pattern broadly (e.g., margin alert -> price change).

Jewelry-vertical data model primitives: serial-number tracking per piece, attachable digital certificates & appraisal records per product, variant attributes (metal type, weight, quality, ring size), barcode + RFID (via partners), layaways & deposits, dedicated Services/Repairs module with per-job assignment & status. These are exactly Zevar's domain and confirm the data shape competitors already support.

Demand-forecasting + suggested-reorder-quantity engine in the Insights module — a real predictive-analytics feature (inventory-side), proving the platform can host ML; Zevar can extend prediction to margin/price.
Insights 'order recommendations' reflect past sales + stockouts — a closed-loop replenishment recommender.
Cross-platform access: web + iOS + Android for reporting; Pulse app for mobile live monitoring.
Pre-built report library (40+ reports per the 2016 launch PR; now larger) categorized into Sales & Inventory, Marketing, Payments, Tax — suggests a curated 'report gallery' UX worth copying.
Multi-location is foundational: outlets/locations are a first-class dimension almost everywhere (filters, dashboards, reports, location-linking for multi-store).

'View by' granularity switcher (day/week/month) bound to a single date picker that auto-formats — reduces dashboard clutter.
Expressive multi-filter builder where same-type filters OR together and different-type filters AND together — predictable, composable, and powerful; document the rule in the UI.
Click 'View report' under any dashboard widget to drill into the full report with the dashboard's filters pre-applied — seamless dashboard->report drill-down (no re-filtering).
'Measure' dropdown on report tables to swap the displayed metric in place (Revenue <-> Items Sold <-> Margin % <-> Avg Sale Value) without re-running — fast metric pivoting.
'Format results' to re-pivot rows by year/weekday/hour and by product/SKU — inline pivot without exporting.
'Comparison' period selector baked into reports and dashboard tiles — period-over-period is one click, not a separate report.
Saved reports + scheduled email delivery (daily/weekly/monthly) — turn any ad-hoc view into a recurring stakeholder deliverable.
Insight-to-action loop: generate a Purchase Order directly from the replenishment/forecast report screen (action where the insight lives).
Leaderboard tables ('Top sales people', 'Top selling items') embedded directly on the operational dashboard — workforce/product ranking without navigation.
Bar charts with hover tooltips showing value + % of total (Sales by Location) — simple, effective for multi-store comparison.
Mobile-first owner app (Pulse) with live sales + historical comparison toggles — for owners away from the counter.
End-of-day Sales Summary email — passive monitoring for non-dashboard users.
data_model_hints: Sale record carries: revenue, tax, discount, line items, payments/tenders, customer link (nullable -> enables capture-rate), register, outlet, user/associate, timestamps (first/last sale). Capture rate = sales WHERE customer_id IS NOT NULL / all sales.

Cost is stored as weighted-average cost per item at time of sale and frozen onto the sale line (so historical COGS/Gross Profit is stable despite later cost changes) — confirmed by Sales by Location doc.
Outlet/Location and Register are first-class entities referenced across sales, inventory, and reporting; multi-store via 'linked locations'.
Product has: SKU, name, category, brand, supplier, tags, variant attributes (e.g., size/metal), serial number, attachable certificates/appraisals (jewelry vertical).
Inventory snapshot per outlet: on-hand, reorder point, reorder amount, days covered, sell-through, dusty flag (no sale within N days), recently-out-of-stock flag.

Replenishment/forecast measures computed as derived columns: Forecasted Demand, Suggested Reorder Quantity, Missed/Estimated items sold (stockout imputation) — implies a forecasting job writing back to the inventory/replenishment table.
User/associate is the workforce dimension keyed off the sale's user field; Individual Performance aggregates measures GROUP BY user.
Saved Report and Report Schedule entities (name, filters, measures, format, schedule, recipients) — a meta-report layer.
Register closure / Z-read entity: opening/closing cash, tender totals, pay-ins/payouts, over/short, open/close user+timestamp.

Demand forecasting + suggested reorder quantity + lost-sale (missed/estimated items) estimation in the Insights module — real predictive analytics on the inventory side, with vendor-published GMROI/stockout benchmarks (marketing, but methodology disclosed).
Customer Capture Rate as a first-class per-employee KPI — a CRM-compliance metric most POS lack, directly relevant to high-value jewelry customer relationships.
Tight insight-to-action loop: build a PO straight from the replenishment report.
Strong period-over-period comparison and saved/scheduled-report ergonomics that lower the analytical effort for managers.
Pulse / Insights Live mobile monitoring gives owners real-time visibility from anywhere.
worth_copying: Inline period-over-period delta arrows on every dashboard tile (S-Series) — low-effort, high-glance-value for Live Monitor.
The same-type=OR / different-type=AND multi-filter rule, surfaced clearly in the UI — adopt as Zevar's universal filter semantics across all 4 modules.
'View report' drill-down that inherits the dashboard's filters — make every Live/Sales widget drill into Profit/Workforce detail without re-filtering.
'Measure' in-place dropdown + 'Format results' inline pivot on report tables — let users re-metric and re-pivot without re-running or exporting.
Saved + Scheduled reports (daily/weekly/monthly auto-email) as a platform primitive — every custom view should be savable and emailable.
Customer Capture Rate as a per-associate and per-store KPI surfaced on the Workforce scoreboard (jewelry CRM compliance is mission-critical).
Sales by Hour of Day report explicitly tied to staffing optimization — feed directly into Workforce scheduling.
Insight-to-action pattern: from a margin/slow-mover/stockout insight, surface a one-click action (create PO, adjust price, transfer stock between stores).
Sales by Location comparison UX (bar charts with hover % of total + sortable/filterable columnar table + export) as the template for the multi-store wall.
Top-N leaderboards (salespeople, products) embedded on operational dashboards.
End-of-day Sales Summary email as a passive monitoring channel for owners.

Forecasted Demand + Suggested Reorder + Lost-sale estimation as the inventory-analytics backbone — extend the same predictive pattern to margin/price (what Zevar's Profit Intelligence needs and Lightspeed lacks).
weaknesses: COGS is a single weighted-average number — no decomposition into gold/gemstone/labor/alloy/making charges. For jewelry, where gold price + making charge + stone cost drive true margin, this is a critical blind spot. Zevar's Profit Intelligence can win decisively here.

Analytics sync lag: Lightspeed Analytics historically synchronized with POS every ~2-3 hours, so 'real-time' was not truly real-time on the retail line (Pulse/Insights Live improves this but is tier-restricted). A genuine sub-minute Live Monitor is a Zevar opportunity.
G2/Capterra users repeatedly call standard reporting 'incomplete and buggy' and say deep custom analytics 'lack when it comes to deep dive' — reporting depth is a known complaint.

Customer support frequently criticized on G2/Capterra/Reddit as slow/unresponsive.
Processor lock-in (Lightspeed Payments) limits flexibility.
Pricing detail is not transparently published (sales-gated) — secondary sources disagree on exact figures, so treat all $ numbers as approximate (unverified).
pricing_model: Tiered SaaS subscription, billed monthly or annually (annual saves ~15-18%). Two product lines with different plan names: R-Series (Basic ~$89-109/mo, Core mid, Plus ~$289-339/mo) and X-Series (Lean/Standard/Insights, starting ~$79/mo). Payments bundled: Lightspeed Payments at a flat ~2.6% + $0.10 card-present across tiers. The Advanced Reporting and Lightspeed Insights analytics are ADD-ONS or gated to higher tiers (Plus/Advanced/Enterprise, or Standard/Lean + Advanced Reporting & Insights add-ons); the real-time Insights Live is gated to the Custom tier. Reported Insights add-on cost is roughly $89-$289/mo (one G2 reviewer cited ~$145/mo for upgraded reports). Lowest pricing typically requires an annual contract. Exact list prices are sales-gated on lightspeedhq.com/pricing, so per-month figures are from secondary aggregators and should be confirmed.

category: General retail POS / omnichannel analytics platform
region: Global (US HQ; multi-language admin); focus here on analytics/relevant to multi-store retail

live_monitoring: Live View (Analytics > Live View): real-time globe + 2D map; blue dots = visitor sessions (active in last 5 min), purple dots = orders; streamer/full-screen modes for wall displays. Metric cards: Visitors right now, Total sales (since midnight), Total sessions, Total orders, Top locations, Customers, Top products, Customer-behavior funnel over last 10 min (Active carts -> Checking out -> Purchased). Designed for BFCM-style high-traffic monitoring and immediate promo/discount impact observation. NOTE: Live View is online-store-centric; for physical POS, real-time per-store walls are weaker and typically served by third parties (Metorik, Report Pundit) — a clear gap Shopify leaves open.

Daily Sales report in POS app itself (POS Next): 3 tiles (Net sales, Average order value, Items per order) each compared to the SAME DAY LAST WEEK; date ranges Today/Yesterday/WTD/MTD/QTD/YTD; lists Top products, Top product types, Top staff at register by net sales, Net payments by type.
sales_monitoring: Retail Sales reports (only POS-channel orders; 1-5 min data latency): POS total sales by product, by product variant SKU, by vendor, by product type, Total sales by POS location, POS total sales by staff member, POS staff daily sales total, POS staff sales total (attributed to line items).
Time dimensions natively available: Day, Day of week (1-7), Hour, Hour of day (0-23), Minute, Week/Month/Quarter/Year + 'of week/year' variants — enables hourly/daily trend slicing out of the box.
Sales-over-time and Average-order-value-over-time reports (AOV formula = (gross sales - discounts) / orders).
Quantity ordered per order (basket depth / UPT proxy) + Items per order tile. Median order value discussed as a gap (AOV only).

Conversion: online conversion rate is native (sessions that completed checkout / sessions) with added-to-cart rate, reached-checkout rate, completed-checkout rate, bounce rate. IN-STORE conversion requires footfall sensors (Shopify POS does NOT count door traffic) — third-party (Hoxton AI, Dor/Aura) needed; conversion = transactions / visitors.
Sell-through rate report, Days of inventory remaining, Days in/out of stock (per location), Percent of inventory sold, Inventory units sold per day — strong slow-mover / fast-mover analytics.
Channel comparison via 'Order sales channel', 'Sales channel', 'Is POS sale' dimensions (Online Store vs POS vs Shop App, etc.) and 'POS location name' for multi-store comparison.
profit_intelligence: Gross profit and Gross margin fields IF COGS is configured (formula: gross profit = net sales - cost of goods sold; gross margin = gross profit / net sales). Net sales = gross sales - discounts - sales reversals.
Cost of goods sold per line item, average COGS, 'Net sales with cost recorded' vs 'Net sales without cost recorded' to flag products missing cost data.
Payment processing fees, average payment processing fees, FX fees, Managed Markets fees — fee-level cost visibility that jewelry tools usually lack.
Discount decomposition: line-item discounts, order-level discounts, shipping discounts, discounts from apps, discount class/method/code/name dimensions — granular markdown analytics.
Returns decomposition: gross returns, net returns, quantity returned, returned quantity rate, return fees, return reason dimension.
Inventory valuation: ending inventory value (at cost) and ending inventory retail value (at price), per location — supports carrying-cost / markdown analysis.

GAP vs jewelry: Shopify COGS is a single static number per SKU. It does NOT model gold spot-price component, gemstone cost, alloy, making/labor charge, or overhead absorption. No native margin simulation / what-if pricing. Dynamic pricing not built in.
workforce_intelligence: Staff PIN login at POS Pro locations; every transaction tied to the logged-in staff member.
'POS total sales by staff member' + 'POS staff daily sales total' + 'POS staff sales total (attributed to sale)' reports; 'Staff member name/ID' and 'Assisting staff member name/ID' dimensions.
'Rate of staff-assisted sales' and 'Percent of sales with staff help' metrics — measures personal-service impact (correlates with higher AOV).

GAP: Single-attribution only natively (one staff per sale). No multi-staff split commission, no built-in commission calculation engine, no per-associate tip breakout, no online-order staff attribution. All require third-party apps (EasyTeam, POS Staff Commission Tracker, Report Pundit). Staff sales attribution is POS Pro only (not Lite).
No native quotas/targets, no gamification, no scheduling/clock-in, no coaching/review features.
cross_cutting: Unified analytics fields model: ~400+ metrics/dimensions across B2B, Customers, Fees, Finance & payments, Fraud, Inventory, Locations, Marketing, Orders, Point of sale, Products, Sales revenue, Sessions, Stores, Time — a self-service report BUILDER with metrics + dimensions + filters + date ranges + 'Save as' custom reports.

Historical-at-time-of-sale dimensions (product title/type/vendor/variant/SKU/price 'at time of sale') so renames/reprices don't corrupt historical reports — critical for jewelry where designs/prices churn.
Product variant ABC grade dimension (A = 80% of revenue, B = 15%, C = 5%) — native ABC analysis at variant level.

Customer cohort analysis (Plus): retention rate, amount spent per customer, cumulative gross/net/total sales, orders per customer, AOV — by cohort month/week/quarter; plus PREDICTIVE spend tier and predictive amount-spent-per-customer.
RFM group dimension (recency/frequency/monetary) available for segmentation; new-or-returning-customer labeling; predicted spend tier.
ShopifyQL / Plus analytics: queryable analytics, custom dashboards, Live View + benchmarks + cohort now in the 'new analytics experience'.
Inventory fields fully location-aware (most metrics have an 'at location' twin) — engineered for multi-store from day one.
Fraud analytics (chargeback rate, fraudulent chargeback rate, fraud risk levels, Shopify Protect eligibility) — relevant to high-ticket jewelry.

worth_copying: Copy the self-service REPORT BUILDER model: a clean separation of metrics (numbers) vs dimensions (group-by) vs filters vs date ranges, with a 'Top 30 helpful fields' onboarding list and 'Save as custom report'. This is the single most powerful idea to port into all 4 Zevar modules — let managers build any view without engineering.

Copy the 'at time of sale' historical-snapshot dimensions for product title/type/vendor/variant SKU/price. Jewelry items get renamed, repriced, and remade constantly; freezing the sale-time values keeps historical reports honest. Implement as immutable line-item snapshot columns.

Copy the Live View pattern for the LIVE MONITOR wall: real-time globe/2D map with color-coded activity dots, big metric cards (visitors/sales/sessions/orders/top products), a 10-minute customer-behavior funnel (active carts -> checking out -> purchased), and a 'Streamer/full-screen' mode for in-store TV displays.

Copy the POS-side DAILY SALES '3 tiles vs same-day-last-week' UX: Net sales / AOV / Items per order, each with a week-over-week comparison, plus Top products / Top product types / Top staff at register / Net payments by type. This is a near-perfect template for an associate-facing daily pulse.

Copy Shopify's rich DISCOUNT & RETURNS decomposition: separate line-item vs order-level vs shipping discounts, discounts-from-apps, return fees, return reasons, returned-quantity-rate filter. Jewelry discounting (making-charge waivers, gold-rate haggling) needs this granularity for true margin work.

Copy the PAYMENT-FEE analytics layer: per-order and average payment-processing fees, FX fees, card-type/BIN/digital-wallet dimensions. Jewelry high-ticket transactions make payment-cost analytics genuinely valuable (unverified per-jewelry-specific processing cost data).

Copy native ABC grade at variant level (A/B/C by revenue contribution) and sell-through rate / days-of-inventory-remaining per location — directly feeds PROFIT INTELLIGENCE slow-mover and markdown decisions.

Copy the Plus COHORT + PREDICTIVE spend model: cohort retention curves, amount-spent-per-customer, orders-per-customer, and a predicted-spend-tier label per customer — high-relevance for jewelry repeat-purchase and bridal/anniversary cycles.
Copy the unified, location-aware inventory data model: nearly every inventory metric has an 'at location' twin so multi-store comparison is first-class, not an afterthought.
Copy 'percent of sales with staff help' / 'rate of staff-assisted sales' — a simple but powerful KPI tying personal service to AOV that most jewelry systems don't surface.
weaknesses: No native in-store footfall / door-traffic counting, so TRUE in-store conversion rate (transactions / visitors) is impossible without third-party sensors (Hoxton AI, Dor/Aura) — a glaring gap Shopify leaves to the ecosystem. Zevar can own footfall+conversion natively.

Live View is built for the ONLINE store (sessions/visitors). There is no equivalent native real-time per-physical-store command wall — owners rely on Metorik/Report Pundit. Clear opening for Zevar's LIVE MONITOR.

COGS is a single flat static value per SKU. No concept of commodity-priced component costs (gold spot, gemstones), labor/making charges, or alloy — so jewelry margins computed in Shopify are inaccurate. No overhead absorption, no what-if pricing, no dynamic pricing.

Workforce: single-staff attribution only; no multi-staff split commission, no commission calc engine, no per-associate tip report, no quotas/targets, no gamification, no scheduling/clock-in, no coaching. Essentially everything in Zevar's WORKFORCE INTELLIGENCE is an unmet need.
Staff sales attribution requires POS Pro (not Lite) — gating a basic analytics need behind a higher tier.

2025 'new dashboard' changes frustrated users (e.g., daily view reduced from 24h to hourly) per Reddit r/shopify — evidence that even mature analytics UX can regress and that stable, owner-friendly defaults matter.
Reports are not true real-time (1-5 min latency on Retail Sales reports) — fine for most, but not a live command-center grade.
RFM scoring and benchmarks not native; cohort/retention require Plus tier or third-party apps (Peel, By the Numbers).
No native repair, layaway, memo/consignment, appraisal, or commission concepts at all — jewelry workflow objects simply don't exist in the data model.
ABC analysis is variant-level by revenue only; no margin-weighted ABC and no days-in-stock + sell-through combined grading natively.
pricing_model: Staff sales attribution & advanced POS analytics require Shopify POS Pro (per-location monthly fee, ~$89/loc/mo in the US) — NOT available on POS Lite. Analytics/Reports and Live View depth scale with Shopify plan (Basic/Shopify/Advanced) and Shopify Plus unlocks custom reports, ShopifyQL, cohorts, predictive metrics, and higher report-volume limits. No per-seat analytics surcharge beyond the plan; most advanced workforce/profit features (commission, RFM, true net profit, multi-store walls) require paid third-party apps.
differentiators: Unified self-service report builder with 400+ documented metrics/dimensions — best-in-class breadth and clarity for non-technical managers
Live View real-time globe/map + funnel — the gold standard 'command center' vibe for an online store
'At time of sale' immutable historical dimensions — mature approach to historical accuracy most POS systems skip
Native ABC grading and a huge location-aware inventory field set (days of stock, sell-through, per-location twins)
Plus cohort + predictive spend/retention analytics built into the platform, not an add-on
Granular discount, returns, and payment-fee decomposition — better fee/markdown transparency than typical jewelry POS
data_model_hints: Metric-vs-dimension architecture: every reportable thing is either a Metric (aggregate, e.g. net_sales) or a Dimension (group-by, e.g. pos_location_name, hour_of_day, staff_member_name). Replicate this as two registries in Zevar and a generic aggregation engine (server-side, MariaDB) so new reports are config, not code.

POS-specific dimensions to add to every sales line: pos_location_id, pos_location_name, pos_register_id, staff_member_id, staff_member_name, assisting_staff_member_id/name, is_pos_sale, sales_channel.

Time dimensions as first-class: day, day_of_week (1=Mon..7=Sun), hour, hour_of_day (0-23), week, week_of_year, month, quarter, year — materialize via a date dimension table for fast hourly/daily trend queries.
Immutable line-item snapshot columns: product_title/type/vendor/variant_sku/variant_title/variant_price all stored 'at time of sale' alongside current live values.

COGS for jewelry should be decomposed (Shopify's single COGS is insufficient): gold_cost (x karat weight x spot), gemstone_cost, alloy_cost, labor_making_charge, overhead_alloc — store as line-item cost components to enable true PROFIT INTELLIGENCE.

Inventory model: ending_inventory_units, starting_inventory_units, days_in_stock, days_out_of_stock, sell_through_rate, days_of_inventory_remaining, ending_inventory_value (at cost), ending_inventory_retail_value (at price) — each with an 'at location' twin.
Customer cohort fields: customer_cohort_month/week/quarter, months/weeks/quarters/years_since_first_purchase, customer_retention_rate, cumulative_net_sales, orders_per_customer, predicted_spend_tier.
Funnel/session model (adapt for physical store with footfall): visitors -> active_carts -> checking_out -> purchased, all over rolling windows (5-10 min for live).
Fee model: payment_processing_fees, fx_fees, plus payment_method, credit_card_type, digital_wallet, payment_card_brand dimensions for payment-cost analytics.
Discount model: discount_class (order/product/shipping), discount_method (code/automatic), discount_type (fixed/percent), discount_value, discount_name/code — enables markdown P&L.

Big metric cards with inline comparison deltas (e.g., vs same day last week) and one-tap 'View report' drill-down
Config-panel report builder: pick metrics + drag/order dimensions + set filters + choose date range + 'Save as'
Three-tile daily pulse (Net sales / AOV / Items per order) on the POS device itself, with date-range chips (Today/Yesterday/WTD/MTD/QTD/YTD)
Category-filtered report library (Retail Sales, Inventory, Finance, Customers) for discoverability

Per-metric 'Top 3' leaderboards (top products, top product types, top staff) on the daily view
confidence: High for all features sourced from official Shopify Help Center docs (POS Analytics, Retail Sales reports, Analytics fields reference, Live View, Sales attribution, Inventory reports) and Shopify Changelog. LIVE MONITOR — Time-range filters + interval slicing (Lightspeed today/week/month; Genesys 30-min intervals).
SALES — Hourly/interval trend tiles with sparklines (Genesys 30-min; Lightspeed trends).

vendor: Retail workforce management & gamification vendors (Deputy, When I Work, UKG, Homebase, Centrical, Axonify, YOOBIC, Nudge, JewelLink, Spiff/CaptivateIQ, SalesScreen/Spinify/Plecto)
category: Workforce Intelligence - competitive teardown & feature synthesis

workforce_intelligence: SCHEDULING & CLOCK-IN (Deputy/When I Work/Homebase/UKG): Drag-and-drop shift scheduling with coverage-vs-demand view; AI/demand-based 'Smart Scheduling' that predicts labor need from POS sales + foot traffic + weather/events (Deputy, Shiftlab, Shyft); auto-scheduling/one-click schedule generation (When I Work); split shifts, role/position-based shifts, availability & time-off management; shift swapping/trading with manager approval; geo-location + face-detection kiosk clock-in (Deputy mobile geo-tag, iPad Kiosk facial recognition); GPS capture on every clock-in/out/break (Homebase); automatic timesheet generation syncing to payroll (ADP, payroll APIs); labor-cost-vs-sales dashboards (sales-to-labor ratio); overtime alerts & compliance; multi-store scheduling wall; mobile-first associate app for viewing schedule, swapping, and notifications.

GAMIFICATION & ENGAGEMENT (Centrical/Gameffective primary reference): Points + custom virtual currency (coins) awarded for KPI achievements and learning; badges for milestones/skills; configurable individual & team leaderboards (store-vs-store championships); 'Missions'/challenges with start/end dates tied to specific business goals (e.g. sell new product line); peer-to-peer personal challenges/bets ('I bet 5 coins I sell 5 first'); smart automated notifications triggered by behavior/context (after Nth sale, push nudge toward goal); virtual rewards store redeeming points for real vouchers/gift cards; social feed of wins/kudos; real-time KPI scoreboards visible to associate on mobile; multi-metric gamification (compete across several KPIs simultaneously); narratives/levels for long-term progression. Reported ROI: +12% productivity, +15% CSAT/FCR, -30% early attrition (Centrical, contact-center data, unverified for jewelry).

LIVE SCOREBOARD / TV DISPLAY (Spinify, Plecto, SalesScreen, Hoopla, Leaderboarded): Real-time ranking on TV/browser for sales floor or break room; auto-updating from POS/CRM data; sports-themed/celebration animations on milestone hits; competitions/sprints (daily/weekly); no-per-seat display pricing; multiple leaderboard views by store/team/individual.

MICROLEARNING & TRAINING (Axonify, YOOBIC, JewelLink Academy): Daily 3-5 question adaptive microlearning bursts; spaced-repetition that adapts to what each employee knows; gamified quizzes awarding points/rare cards; product-knowledge launches tied to new inventory/promotions; video-pitch submission tasks for manager feedback; onboarding milestone tracking (product/bridal/inventory knowledge benchmarks); mobile-first; 83% engagement rate claimed (Axonify, unverified).

COACHING & CLIENTELING (JewelLink is the jewelry-native reference; Centrical Coaching): AI-powered 1-on-1 coaching prompts; manager 'Team Console' showing who completed training vs who hasn't sold yet; pre-populated nudge templates (request training, invite for 15-min coffee); sales-pitch video review tasks; clienteling as relationship ecosystem: exposure/contact/continued-access model; appointment scheduling & tracking (bridal consultations, timepiece allocations); follow-up task assignment & overdue-follow-up alerts; lead routing (bridal inquiries, timepiece waitlists); customer timeline unifying POS history + texting + notes + appointments; 'book of business' per associate with relationship limits (jewelers cite ~5-10 deep relationships per associate); personality-trait evaluation + AI customer-practice roleplay (JewelLink).

PERFORMANCE & QUARTERLY REVIEWS (UKG Pro Performance, Centrical Performance Mgmt, Zevar existing): Weighted goal/scorecard model (revenue/activity/quality weights); SMART goals with weighting/prioritization (UKG); auto-generated strengths & improvement insights per quarter; performance tier + QoQ change; compensation-impact recommendations (recommended rate, bonus); development plans; employee acknowledgment workflow; scheduled auto-generation of reviews. NOTE: Zevar already implements most of this in performance_target / quarterly_performance_review doctypes - this is an area of relative strength.

COMMISSION AUTOMATION (Spiff, CaptivateIQ, QuotaPath, POS-built-in): Automatic commission calc from POS transactions in real time; tiered rules + SPIFFs (flat bonuses per unit/category); multi-payee commission splits on shared sales (Zevar has sales_commission_split); commission dashboards/estimates visible to associate; attainment vs quota progress bars; variable compensation by category/margin (jewelry-specific: different rates for bridal vs repair vs gold-purchase).
cross_cutting: Mobile-first associate app: schedule, scoreboard, learning, kudos, chat in one app (Deputy, When I Work, YOOBIC, Centrical all converge here).
Push-notification engine with behavioral triggers (not just schedule reminders) - Centrical's 'after 5th sale nudge' pattern.
Open API / webhook integration with POS so performance data flows automatically - CaptivateIQ/Spiff pull from CRM/POS; Zevar already hooks Sales Invoice events via log_sale_event.

Data model pattern: a generic 'performance event log' (event_type, employee, revenue_amount, item_count, hours_worked, custom_data JSON) fed by many source doctypes - Zevar's performance_log already follows this; competitors reinforce it as the right abstraction.
Sales-to-labor ratio as a north-star cross-cutting KPI (jewelry benchmark: keep <=15% per JewelLink) - sits at the intersection of Sales Monitor, Profit Intelligence, and Workforce.
Gamification must be tied to business KPIs with thoughtful narrative, not slapped-on leaderboards (Centrical's own cautionary guidance) - relevant design principle for Zevar.
profit_intelligence: Sales-to-salary ratio target <=15% for jewelry store profitability (JewelLink benchmark) - bridges workforce cost into profit intelligence.
Labor-cost-as-%-of-sales dashboards from scheduling tools (Deputy/Homebase) feed directly into overhead absorption & margin analysis.

Compensation tied to true margin not just revenue: opportunity to commission on gross-margin dollars (CaptivateIQ/Spiff support margin-based plans) - especially relevant for jewelry where gold/gemstone COGS varies wildly per item.
sales_monitoring: Sales-to-labor ratio and conversion-per-labor-hour as cross-cutting KPIs surfaced by UKG/Deputy labor forecasting vs actuals.
Hourly sales curve overlaid with staffing curve (Deputy/Shiftlab demand-forecast visualization) - reveals under/over-staffed hours driving lost conversions.
Per-associate contribution to hourly/daily sales trends (Centrical KPI scoreboards) feeds Sales Monitor's associate dimension.
live_monitoring: Live employee activity feed: clock-ins/outs, current shift status, who is on floor (Homebase/Deputy live; Zevar has employee_live_monitor.py already).
Live sales scoreboard on a store wall display updating in real time (Spinify/Plecto/Leaderboarded TV mode) - directly feeds the multi-store wall vision.
Live anomaly/alert layer: associate not hitting goals mid-shift triggers manager nudge (Centrical behavioral notifications) - maps to Live Monitor alerts.

kpis: Per-associate & per-store: Revenue ($), Revenue vs target (% attainment), ATV/AOV (jewelry benchmark ~$1,823), transactions count, items sold, UPT (units per transaction), high-ticket count (above configurable threshold)
Conversion rate (jewelry target ~25%) and conversion-per-labor-hour (traffic vs sales vs staff on floor)
Customers served, new vs returning, layaway conversions, repair orders taken, upsell rate %
Commission earned ($) and commission vs quota progress; SPIFFs earned; sales-to-salary ratio (target <=15%)
Attendance rate %, punctuality score, average hours/week, no-shows, late clock-ins
Sales-to-labor ratio (% ), labor cost vs sales, overtime hours/cost, schedule adherence vs forecast
Quality KPIs: return rate max %, layaway default rate max %, customer satisfaction %
Gamification KPIs: points earned, leaderboard rank, challenges completed, badges unlocked, learning completion %, streak days

Performance tier distribution across team, QoQ change, early-attrition risk score (Centrical targets -30% early attrition)
Microlearning: knowledge-retention score, daily participation rate, time-to-proficiency for new hires (Axonify reports 83% engagement)
ux_patterns: Drag-and-drop schedule grid (Deputy/When I Work): shifts as blocks on employee-x-time axis, coverage bar at top showing scheduled hours vs forecast demand; color-coded by role/position.
Coverage-vs-demand visualization: forecast sales curve as a line, scheduled labor hours as bars beneath - instantly reveals over/under-staffed periods (Shiftlab/Shyft pattern).
Mobile clock-in with geo-fence + optional selfie/face detection (Deputy Kiosk); one-tap clock-in/out/break on associate phone.
Live TV scoreboard (Spinify/Plecto): big-number leaderboards, avatar + rank, animated celebration popups when someone hits a milestone; auto-rotating slides for multiple metrics.
Personal associate dashboard (Centrical): my KPIs today with progress rings/bars to goal, my points/coins, my badges, my rank on leaderboard, 'next mission' card, redeem-rewards button.

Manager Team Console (Centrical/JewelLink): heatmap/list of team showing training-completed, sales-this-period, goals-attainment; one-click action menu per associate (nudge, assign task, invite to 1:1, send feedback template).

Behavioral push notifications with deep-links: 'Your team needs you - you've sold 5 iPhones, perfect FLEX candidates, tap to learn how to sell FLEX' -> links to microlearning (Centrical campaign pattern).

differentiators: Jewelry-native compensation model: Zevar already models tiered hourly rates tied to weighted performance + guaranteed/target/superior bands + minimum & termination thresholds - none of the generic WFM/gamification tools understand jewelry's blend of hourly + commission + high-ticket SPIFFs + repair commissions. This is a real moat.

Multi-category commission awareness (sales vs gold-purchase vs repair vs layaway) - Zevar has commission_rule, repair_commission_settings, sales_commission_split; generic tools treat 'a sale' uniformly.

JewelLink is the only true jewelry-native competitor in this space and is mostly a clienteling/CRM/coaching bolt-on to existing POS. Zevar can be the first NATIVE workforce+clienteling+gamification layer inside a jewelry ERP, not a bolt-on.
Deep product knowledge specificity: bridal workflows, timepiece allocations, gemstone/metal COGS-aware commissioning - generic retail gamification cannot model this.

Integration of LIVE performance (live_monitor + employee_live_monitor already exist) with gamification nudges mid-shift - most gamification tools are batch/periodic; Zevar can nudge in real-time because it owns the POS event stream.
worth_copying: Centrical's behavior-triggered smart notifications: don't just show a leaderboard - after an associate's Nth sale, push a context-aware nudge ('you just sold 5 high-margin items, you're 2 away from today's bonus'). Build this on top of Zevar's existing log_sale_event hook.
Centrical virtual rewards store with point redemption for real gift cards/vouchers - the closing loop that makes points meaningful. Add a 'Rewards Catalog' doctype + redemption flow.

Centrical Team Manager Console: one screen showing every associate's training status, sales-this-period, and goal attainment with one-click actions (nudge / assign / invite to 1:1). High-leverage manager UX.
Centrical multi-metric gamification + team-vs-team championship competitions (store-vs-store) - jewelry is multi-store by nature, this is a natural fit.

Deputy/Homebase demand-based smart scheduling: forecast labor from Zevar's own pos_session sales history (Zevar owns this data - no integration needed), show coverage-vs-demand, auto-generate shifts. Build 'Labor Forecast' nightly job.
Deputy geo-fenced + facial-recognition kiosk clock-in - prevents buddy-punching, relevant for jewelry security. iPad kiosk mode at store entrance.
Spinify/Plecto TV scoreboard mode for the store floor / break room - low effort, high motivational impact; auto-rotate revenue / ATV / UPT / commission-attainment leaderboards.

JewelLink clienteling-as-ecosystem model: explicit 'book of business' per associate with relationship-tier tracking and the 5-10 deep-relationship capacity concept - quantify and surface each associate's active high-value relationships.
JewelLink follow-up accountability: overdue follow-up alerts surface idle opportunities to managers ('Emily completed training but hasn't sold'); map to Zevar's follow-up tasks + manager console.
Axonify adaptive spaced-repetition microlearning tied to new-inventory launches - when new bridal/gemstone stock arrives, auto-push a 3-question quiz worth points.
Spiff/CaptivateIQ real-time commission attainment bars visible to the associate on their phone ('you're at 78% of this month's $4k commission target') - drive behavior with transparency.
Personal peer-to-peer challenges/bets (Centrical 'I bet 5 coins I outsell you') - lightweight, viral engagement among associates.
weaknesses: Centrical/Axonify/YOOBIC are generic and have ZERO jewelry-domain model - they cannot natively handle gold-purchase commissions, bridal lead workflows, gemstone COGS-based margin commissioning, or repair commissions. Zevar's domain model is the opening.

Most gamification platforms sit OUTSIDE the POS and rely on brittle integrations/batch sync (CaptivateIQ pulls from CRM nightly). Because Zevar owns the POS event stream it can do real-time, reliable gamification - a structural advantage.

Generic scheduling tools (Deputy/When I Work/Homebase) don't understand jewelry sales cycles (bridal season, holiday spikes, appointment-driven traffic) without heavy configuration; Zevar can bake jewelry seasonality into forecasting.

Centrical itself cautions that points/leaderboards slapped onto KPIs without narrative design drive undesired behaviors (gaming metrics, cutting corners) - a risk to design around; jewelry sales integrity (no discount-padding to hit targets) matters more than in cell-phone retail.

Gamification reported ROI figures (12% productivity, 30% attrition reduction, 83% engagement) are almost all from contact-center or generic retail contexts - NOT verified for jewelry; treat as directional only.

Generic tools risk commoditizing the associate on pure leaderboard rank; jewelry selling is relationship-driven (JewelLink's exposure/contact/access thesis) - pure leaderboards can backfire by encouraging transaction-chasing over relationship-building. Zevar should weight relationship/clienteling KPIs alongside revenue in gamification.
pricing_model: Workforce/gamification tools: per-user-per-month SaaS. Deputy ~$4.50-9/user/mo (Premium/Enterprise); When I Work ~$3-6/user/mo; Homebase free tier + ~$20-70/mo per location for essentials; UKG Pro WFM enterprise (quote-based, typically $/employee/yr, enterprise scale). Gamification: Centrical/Axonify/YOOBIC enterprise quote-based (~$5-15+/user/mo estimated, unverified). Leaderboard TV tools: Spinify/Plecto/SalesScreen ~$5-15/user/mo or flat per-display. Commission automation: Spiff/CaptivateIQ enterprise quote-based. JewelLink: jewelry-specific pricing not public (likely per-store/mo). Note: these are standalone-tool prices - Zevar's advantage is bundling all of this natively, so per-store ERP pricing with workforce module included is the strategic play.

Medium for KPI benchmarks (jewelry-specific figures like ~$1,823 AOV, ~25% conversion, <=15% sales-to-salary ratio come from jewelry trade sources but vary by store; treat as directional). High for Zevar's existing data-model assessment (directly read from the codebase doctypes and performance.py API).
